vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1402: Work agent state.json.claudeSessionId stays null — heartbeat hook never records the Claude session

plan.title: Restore PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop hook registration in global settings.json (revert PAN-982)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: For every new work agent (e.g. agent-pan-1132), the Claude Code JSONL transcript exists on disk but ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/sessions.json and activity.jsonl are never created, the runtime snapshot reports claudeSessionId as null, and the dashboard's Command Deck Session pane shows 'No conversation data available'. Resume-after-stop also fails with 'No saved session ID found'. Root cause: PAN-982 moved PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop hook registrations out of ~/.claude/settings.json and into per-agent frontmatter at roles/<role>.md (and sync-sources/agents/pan-*-agent.md) on the assumption Claude Code honors frontmatter hooks when the agent is loaded via `--agent`. The launcher invokes `claude --agent roles/work.md` — a file path rather than the agent-name lookup Claude Code's CLI reference documents. Whether Claude silently drops frontmatter hooks for path-form invocations or refuses to load the agent at all, the observable effect is the same: heartbeat-hook/pre-tool-hook/stop-hook never fire, no sessions.json or activity.jsonl is written, and the dashboard's session pane is empty for every running work agent.
narratives.Proposal: Revert PAN-982. Restore the pre-migration registrations in setupHooksCommand (src/cli/commands/setup/hooks.ts) so PreToolUse:pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse:heartbeat-hook + permission-event-hook, Stop:stop-hook + permission-event-hook, and the python3-gated TLDR helpers are written back into ~/.claude/settings.json on `pan install`. Delete the matching `removeIfPresent(...)` prune block. Strip the now-redundant `hooks:` blocks from roles/*.md (5 files) and sync-sources/agents/pan-*-agent.md (7 files) so settings.json is the single source of truth and there is zero double-fire risk regardless of whether Claude Code ever starts honoring `--agent <path>` frontmatter. Add a regression test in setup-hooks.test.ts that asserts the tool-event hooks ARE registered after setup. Update docs/HOOKS.md to describe the post-PAN-1402 state (all hooks live globally, with a 'History' paragraph explaining the PAN-982 attempt and the PAN-1402 revert).

autoDecisions.summary: Revert PAN-982 by restoring hooks to settings.json rather than fixing Claude Code's path-form `--agent` to load frontmatter hooks.
autoDecisions.rationale: Restoring known-working pre-PAN-982 behavior is the smallest change that fixes the bug. Investigating/fixing whatever Claude Code does with `--agent <path>` is upstream work outside Panopticon's control; even if it could be fixed, the revert removes Panopticon's dependency on the undocumented path-form behavior entirely.

autoDecisions.summary: Strip `hooks:` from both roles/*.md AND sync-sources/agents/pan-*-agent.md.
autoDecisions.rationale: Required to eliminate the H4 double-fire hazard from PAN-982 in case Claude Code ever starts honoring frontmatter hooks via path. Removing the duplication makes settings.json the only source of truth and matches the no-bandaids rule (one canonical declaration, not two).

autoDecisions.summary: Do NOT in this issue migrate the launcher to `--agent <name>` lookups from `.claude/agents/`.
autoDecisions.rationale: That would be a separate, larger restructure (rename `name:` in role files, sync to `.claude/agents/` reliably, update getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand, update tests). It's orthogonal to the heartbeat-hook bug. File a follow-up if the team wants Panopticon to use Claude Code's named-agent mechanism end-to-end.

autoDecisions.summary: Run `pan install` (idempotent) as the user-facing migration; do not write a separate migration script.
autoDecisions.rationale: setupHooksCommand already uses addHookIfMissing, which is the standard upgrade path. A bespoke migration script would duplicate logic and add a one-time-use code path. Document the requirement to re-run `pan install` after upgrading in HOOKS.md.

autoDecisions.summary: Replace the role-definitions.test.ts `frontmatter.hooks` assertions with a regression test in setup-hooks.test.ts rather than keeping the frontmatter assertion.
autoDecisions.rationale: After the strip, role frontmatter has no `hooks:` field, so the old assertion is wrong. The new home for the guarantee — 'tool-event hooks are registered globally' — is the install code path, which setup-hooks.test.ts already exercises.

items.title: Re-register PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop hooks in setupHooksCommand and remove the PAN-982 prune block

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/setup/hooks.ts, restore the pre-PAN-982 registrations: addHookIfMissing('PreToolUse', 'pre-tool-hook'), addHookIfMissing('PostToolUse', 'heartbeat-hook'), addHookIfMissing('PostToolUse', 'permission-event-hook'), addHookIfMissing('Stop', 'stop-hook'), addHookIfMissing('Stop', 'permission-event-hook'), and (inside the existing `if (python3Available)` gate) addHookIfMissing('PreToolUse', 'tldr-read-enforcer', 'Read') + addHookIfMissing('PostToolUse', 'tldr-post-edit', 'Edit|Write'). Delete the entire `removeIfPresent(...)` block (lines 384–406 of the current file) including the 'PAN-982: Atomic migration' comment. Rewrite the PAN-982 explanatory comment (lines 352–369) into a shorter PAN-1402 note explaining that tool-event hooks were briefly migrated to per-agent frontmatter and reverted because the path-form `--agent` did not honor them. Update the success-message `console.log` block (lines 452–464) to list the restored hooks alongside the existing six. The bead lands cleanly only when `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm test src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts` all pass.

subItems.title: After running setupHooksCommand on an empty settings.json, the result contains PreToolUse with pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse with heartbeat-hook + permission-event-hook, and Stop with stop-hook + permission-event-hook.
When python3 is detected, the post-setup settings.json also contains PreToolUse with tldr-read-enforcer (matcher 'Read') and PostToolUse with tldr-post-edit (matcher 'Edit|Write').

The `removeIfPresent(...)` PAN-982 migration block (and its console.log of removed entries) is fully deleted; setupHooksCommand never strips PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop entries that point at Panopticon's bin dir.
Running setupHooksCommand twice in a row leaves settings.json byte-identical the second time (idempotency preserved by addHookIfMissing).
`npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm test -- src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts` all pass with no new failures after the change.

items.title: Remove `hooks:` blocks from roles/*.md and drop the matching assertions in role-definitions.test.ts

narrative.Action: Delete the `hooks:` YAML block from the frontmatter of all five role files: roles/work.md, roles/plan.md, roles/review.md, roles/test.md, roles/ship.md. Keep every other frontmatter field (name, description, permissionMode, effort, tools, mcpServers) untouched. In src/lib/__tests__/role-definitions.test.ts, drop every `expect(frontmatter.hooks).toEqual(expect.any(Object))` assertion (lines 33, 57, 97, 116, 137). The role files themselves remain in roles/ — only the hooks declaration goes.

subItems.title: roles/work.md, roles/plan.md, roles/review.md, roles/test.md, and roles/ship.md no longer contain a `hooks:` key in their YAML frontmatter.
All other frontmatter fields (name, description, permissionMode, effort, tools, mcpServers) on the five role files are preserved exactly as before.

narrative.Action: Delete the `hooks:` YAML block from the frontmatter of all seven Panopticon pipeline agent definitions under sync-sources/agents/: pan-work-agent.md, pan-planning-agent.md, pan-review-agent.md, pan-inspect-agent.md, pan-test-agent.md, pan-uat-agent.md, pan-merge-agent.md. Keep every other frontmatter field (name, description, model, permissionMode, effort, tools, mcpServers) untouched. These files are sync sources — the workspace `.claude/agents/` directory copies them on `pan install` / `pan sync`. After this strip, the agents (if Claude Code ever discovers them by name) carry no frontmatter hooks, eliminating any future double-fire pathway.

subItems.title: sync-sources/agents/pan-work-agent.md, pan-planning-agent.md, pan-review-agent.md, pan-inspect-agent.md, pan-test-agent.md, pan-uat-agent.md, and pan-merge-agent.md no longer contain a `hooks:` key in their YAML frontmatter.
All other frontmatter fields (name, description, model, permissionMode, effort, tools, mcpServers) on the seven agent files are preserved exactly as before.
Any existing test that asserts `frontmatter.hooks` exists for these agent files is updated or removed; `npm test` passes.

items.title: Add regression tests in setup-hooks.test.ts that assert tool-event hooks register globally

narrative.Action: Extend src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts with new unit tests that exercise the helper-level surface (`addPanopticonHookIfMissing` on a fresh ClaudeSettings) for each restored entry: PreToolUse:pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse:heartbeat-hook, PostToolUse:permission-event-hook, Stop:stop-hook, Stop:permission-event-hook, PreToolUse:tldr-read-enforcer ('Read'), PostToolUse:tldr-post-edit ('Edit|Write'). Each test asserts both (a) the first call returns true and writes the expected matcher + command, and (b) a duplicate call is a no-op. The intent is a regression net: if anyone re-introduces a PAN-982-style strip in setupHooksCommand, these tests still pass at the helper layer, so add a higher-level test too that invokes the full setupHooksCommand with a temp HOME / .claude directory and asserts each of the six entries lands on disk. Use existing test patterns in the file.

subItems.title: setup-hooks.test.ts contains a regression test for each tool-event hook restored in restore-tool-event-hooks-in-settings, asserting addPanopticonHookIfMissing writes the entry and is idempotent on the second call.

A higher-level test exercises setupHooksCommand against an isolated temp HOME and asserts the resulting settings.json contains PreToolUse:pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse:heartbeat-hook, PostToolUse:permission-event-hook, Stop:stop-hook, and Stop:permission-event-hook (TLDR entries conditional on python3 detection).
`npm test -- src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts` passes with the new assertions.

items.title: Update docs/HOOKS.md to describe the PAN-1402 revert and remove the split-registry section

narrative.Action: Rewrite the 'Split-Registry Rule (PAN-982)' section of docs/HOOKS.md (lines 23–48). Replace it with a 'Hook Registration' section explaining all nine hook events live in global ~/.claude/settings.json and are installed by `pan install` / `pan admin hooks install`. Add a short 'History' note: PAN-982 attempted a per-agent frontmatter migration for PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop; PAN-1402 reverted it because Claude Code did not honor the frontmatter hooks when `--agent` was passed a file path. Update the 'Migration Pruning' subsection to point at the new PAN-1402 state (no pruning needed; the prune block was removed). Leave the rest of the document (hook inventory, execution flow, Pi gaps) unchanged.

subItems.title: docs/HOOKS.md no longer contains the 'Split-Registry Rule (PAN-982)' section claiming PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop live in per-agent frontmatter.
A new 'Hook Registration' section describes all nine hooks living in global settings.json, with a 'History' paragraph summarizing the PAN-982 attempt and PAN-1402 revert.
Users upgrading across PAN-1402 are explicitly told to re-run `pan install` and restart any in-flight work agents to pick up the restored hook registration.
